perturbation.original_text: 5. No Disclosure. The RECEIVING PARTY and its employees, officers, agents and affiliates shall holdthe CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION in confidence and take all reasonable steps to preserve theconfidential and proprietary nature of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, including, withoutlimitation: (i) refraining from disclosing the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to persons within itsorganization not having a reason to know, and all persons outside its organization unless theyhave a reason to know and are bound by fiduciary duties of confidentiality to the RECEIVING PARTY;and (ii) advising all of its employees, officers, agents and affiliates who gain access to theCONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION of its confidential and proprietary nature. The obligations of thisparagraph also apply to the fact of the existence of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, of thisAgreement, and the occurrence of all meetings and communications of the parties that involveCONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. In the event the RECEIVING PARTY receives in writing an opinion from itsoutside counsel that it must disclose certain information, prohibited by the terms herein, in orderto avoid committing a violation of law, the RECEIVING PARTY may disclose only that informationnecessary to avoid committing such violation of the law.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. No Disclosure. The RECEIVING PARTY and its employees, officers, agents and affiliates shall holdthe CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION in confidence and take all reasonable steps to preserve theconfidential and proprietary nature of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, including, withoutlimitation: (i) refraining from disclosing the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to persons within itsorganization not having a reason to know, and all persons outside its organization; and (ii) advising all of its employees, officers, agents and affiliates who gain access to theCONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION of its confidential and proprietary nature. The obligations of thisparagraph also apply to the fact of the existence of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, of thisAgreement, and the occurrence of all meetings and communications of the parties that involveCONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. In the event the RECEIVING PARTY receives in writing an opinion from itsoutside counsel that it must disclose certain information, prohibited by the terms herein, in orderto avoid committing a violation of law, the RECEIVING PARTY may disclose only that informationnecessary to avoid committing such violation of the law.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction by removing the condition that external persons must be bound by fiduciary duties of confidentiality to the Receiving Party before confidential information can be disclosed to them. This weakens the protection of the Disclosing Party's confidential information and contradicts the intent of preserving its confidential nature, as now external parties without any confidentiality obligations can have access.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. No Disclosure. The RECEIVING PARTY and its employees, officers, agents and affiliates shall holdthe CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION in confidence and take all reasonable steps to preserve theconfidential and proprietary nature of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, including, withoutlimitation: (i) refraining from disclosing the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to persons within itsorganization not having a reason to know, and all persons outside its organization; and (ii) advising all of its employees, officers, agents and affiliates who gain access to theCONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION of its confidential and proprietary nature. The obligations of thisparagraph also apply to the fact of the existence of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, of thisAgreement, and the occurrence of all meetings and communications of the parties that involveCONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. In the event the RECEIVING PARTY receives in writing an opinion from itsoutside counsel that it must disclose certain information, prohibited by the terms herein, in orderto avoid committing a violation of law, the RECEIVING PARTY may disclose only that informationnecessary to avoid committing such violation of the law.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires external parties to be bound by fiduciary duties of confidentiality before receiving confidential information. The changed text removes this requirement, allowing disclosure to external parties without any confidentiality obligations. This directly contradicts the purpose of preserving the confidentiality of the information as stated in the paragraph itself and creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION: AG Projects products and services.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION: AG Projects products and services, except publicly available information.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction. While section 8 already specifies exceptions to confidentiality, adding a specific exception here directly conflicts with the general definition of confidential information. It's not clear whether 'publicly available information' refers only to AG Projects products and services, or all information related to the agreement. This undermines the clause that the agreement is protecting the products and services.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Limitation. The confidentiality and non-disclosure obligations of the previous paragraphs shallnot apply if, and to the extent that: the RECEIVING PARTY can prove that theCONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION was known to the RECEIVING PARTY prior to itsreceipt from the DISCLOSING PARTY (ii) the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is or becomes part ofthe public domain other than by the fault of the RECEIVING PARTY; or (iii) the CONFIDENTIALINFORMATION is rightfully disclosed to the RECEIVING PARTY by a third party that is legally free todisclose such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.justification: The original text broadly defines 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' as 'AG Projects products and services.' The changed text adds 'except publicly available information,' creating a potential conflict with section 8. Section 8 already defines specific limitations to confidentiality. By adding an additional exception within the definition itself, it creates ambiguity about whether 'publicly available information' is covered under section 8's exceptions or represents an entirely separate exclusion. This undermines the confidentiality of AG Projects products and services, which conflicts with section 8's intention to define clear exceptions to confidentiality, thus creating an inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: 15. Governing law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the lawsof Netherlands, excluding its rules for choice of law. Notwithstanding an action for injunction orother equitable remedy, all disputes relating to or arising out of or in connection with thisAgreement shall be finally settled by arbitration in accordance with the Rules of the ArbitrationInstitute of the Amsterdam Chamber of Commerce. The arbitration shall be held in theNetherlands, Amsterdam and the English language shall be used in the proceedings. Thearbitration decision shall be final and binding on the parties and enforceable in any court ofcompetent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. Governing law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the lawsof Netherlands, including its rules for choice of law. Notwithstanding an action for injunction orother equitable remedy, all disputes relating to or arising out of or in connection with thisAgreement shall be finally settled by litigation in the courts of Netherlands. The arbitration decision shall be final and binding on the parties and enforceable in any court ofcompetent jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction because it specifies the agreement shall be governed by the laws of Netherlands, including its rules for choice of law and that disputes will be settled by litigation, but the text ends by claiming the decision is final and binding in arbitration which is an entirely different dispute resolution method.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15. Governing law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the lawsof Netherlands, including its rules for choice of law. Notwithstanding an action for injunction orother equitable remedy, all disputes relating to or arising out of or in connection with thisAgreement shall be finally settled by litigation in the courts of Netherlands. The arbitration decision shall be final and binding on the parties and enforceable in any court ofcompetent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the dispute resolution method from arbitration to litigation while simultaneously stating that 'The arbitration decision shall be final and binding'. This creates a direct contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, highlighting the in-text inconsistency created by retaining the arbitration clause after changing the primary dispute resolution mechanism to litigation. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition provided.
